# Tabs Component Documentation

Data-driven, accessible Tabs component for Svelte 5.

## Why Tabs?

### Data-First Design
"Components automatically understand and adapt to data structures without requiring data transformation."

### Flexible Layout Options
- Horizontal and vertical orientations
- Configurable tab bar positioning (before/after)
- Alignment control (start/center/end)

### Developer Experience
- Full TypeScript support
- Runes-based reactivity
- Keyboard navigation with ListController
- Custom snippets for advanced rendering

### Accessibility Features
- WAI-ARIA compliant
- Full keyboard navigation
- Screen reader support
- Semantic HTML structure

## Component API

### Props
- `items: any[]` - Array of tab items (strings or objects)
- `value: any` - Selected tab value (bindable)
- `fields: FieldMapping` - Field mappings for data extraction
- `orientation: 'horizontal' | 'vertical'` - Layout direction
- `position: 'before' | 'after'` - Tab bar position relative to content
- `align: 'start' | 'center' | 'end'` - Tab alignment within bar
- `editable: boolean` - Enable add/remove functionality
- `placeholder: string` - Text shown when no tab selected
- `tabindex: number` - Tab index for keyboard navigation
- `name: string` - Name for accessibility (aria-label)

### Events
- `onselect: (data) => void` - Fired when tab is selected
- `onchange: (data) => void` - Fired when selected tab changes
- `onmove: (data) => void` - Fired when focus moves between tabs
- `onadd: () => void` - Fired when add button clicked (editable mode)
- `onremove: (item) => void` - Fired when tab is removed (editable mode)

### Snippets
- `child: (item) => void` - Custom tab header rendering
- `empty: () => void` - Empty state when no tabs available

Note: Content between the component tags renders as tab content (like default slot in Svelte 4).

## Usage Examples

### Basic String Array
```svelte
<script>
  import { Tabs } from '@rokkit/ui'
  
  let items = ['Fruits', 'Vegetables', 'Nuts']
  let value = $state(items[0])
</script>

<Tabs {items} bind:value>
  <div class="p-4">
    <h3>Selected: {value}</h3>
    <p>Content for {value}</p>
  </div>
</Tabs>
```

### Object Array with Field Mapping
```svelte
<script>
  import { Tabs } from '@rokkit/ui'
  
  let items = [
    { id: 1, name: 'Dashboard', content: 'Analytics overview' },
    { id: 2, name: 'Users', content: 'User management' }
  ]
  let value = $state(items[0])
</script>

<Tabs {items} bind:value fields={{ text: 'name' }}>
  <div class="p-4">
    <h3>{value.name}</h3>
    <p>{value.content}</p>
  </div>
</Tabs>
```

### Vertical Layout with Custom Snippets
```svelte
<Tabs {items} bind:value orientation="vertical">
  {#snippet child(item)}
    <div class="flex items-center gap-2">
      <span>{item.get('emoji')}</span>
      <span>{item.get('text')}</span>
    </div>
  {/snippet}
  
  <div class="p-4">
    <h3>{value.text}</h3>
    <p>{value.description}</p>
  </div>
</Tabs>
```

### Editable Tabs
```svelte
<Tabs 
  {items} 
  bind:value 
  editable={true}
  placeholder="Add your first tab"
  onadd={handleAddTab}
  onremove={handleRemoveTab}
>
  <!-- Content renders here -->
</Tabs>
```

## Data Attributes for Styling

### Container Attributes
- `data-tab-root` - Root container
- `data-tab-orientation` - Orientation value
- `data-tab-position` - Position value
- `data-tab-align` - Alignment value

### Element Attributes
- `data-tab-list` - Tab bar container
- `data-tab-item` - Individual tab button
- `data-tab-content` - Content container
- `data-tab-add` - Add button (editable mode)
- `data-tab-remove` - Remove button (editable mode)

## Field Mapping

The `fields` prop allows adaptation to any data structure:

```javascript
const fields = {
  text: 'name',        // Map 'name' to tab label
  content: 'body',     // Map 'body' to tab content
  image: 'thumbnail'   // Map 'thumbnail' to tab image
}
```

## Best Practices

### UX Guidelines
- Keep tab labels concise and descriptive
- Use 5-7 tabs maximum for optimal usability
- Provide clear visual feedback for active states
- Consider vertical layout for navigation-heavy interfaces

### Technical Tips
- Use field mapping to adapt to your data structure
- Implement proper error handling for dynamic content
- Test keyboard navigation thoroughly
- Consider lazy loading for heavy tab content

## Community

Part of the Rokkit UI library ecosystem.

Contributions welcome:
- [GitHub Repository](https://github.com/jerrythomas/rokkit)
- [Documentation](https://rokkit.dev)

## Acknowledgments

Built with modern Svelte 5 features:
- Runes for reactivity
- Snippets for customization
- Actions for behaviors
- TypeScript for type safety
